sentence_reviews.text: Relative similarity or ordering is optimized instead of only class probability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DPO abstract lines 41-44 describes relative-quality labels and preference optimization; PML source covers ML objectives generally.
sentence_reviews.notes: Ranking and metric objectives optimize comparisons or distances; the statement combines several objective families.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Margin, ranking & metric objectives is to connect Supervised learning, Preference learning to downstream capabilities including Reward modeling, External retrieval & ranking, Support vector & kernel machines.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DPO abstract lines 41-44; RLHF abstract lines 37-39; PML landing page lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sources support preference/reward modeling and general ML foundations, but not the exact graph bridge or all downstream nodes.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DPO abstract lines 41-44; RLHF abstract lines 37-39 describes rankings used for human-feedback fine-tuning.
sentence_reviews.notes: Preference/ranking data are directly used to train or parameterize reward models in these sources.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CLIP abstract lines 42-43 describes matching captions to images and learned representations from image-text pairs.
sentence_reviews.notes: Contrastive/ranking-style objectives support retrieval, but this exact external-retrieval node relation is inferred.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Support vector & kernel machines
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, classical ML coverage lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: SVMs use margin objectives; the inspected PML excerpt supports classical ML coverage but not the exact claim directly.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Supervised learning, Preference learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DPO abstract lines 41-45; RLHF abstract lines 37-40.
sentence_reviews.notes: Preference-learning dependency is supported, but the surrogate limitation is not directly evidenced by the inspected source locations.

sentence_reviews.text: Margin, ranking & metric objectives uses Supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML landing page lines 45-48 describes classical and modern ML foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Some margin/ranking objectives are supervised, but unsupervised or self-supervised contrastive variants exist.

sentence_reviews.text: Margin, ranking & metric objectives uses Preference learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DPO abstract lines 41-44; RLHF abstract lines 37-39.
sentence_reviews.notes: Ranking objectives are central in preference learning, but not every margin or metric objective uses preference learning.

sentence_reviews.text: Margin, ranking & metric objectives uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CLIP abstract lines 42-43 describes learning image representations by matching captions to images.
sentence_reviews.notes: Metric and contrastive objectives often operate in embedding spaces; not all ranking objectives require embeddings.

edge_reviews.notes: Supervision is common but not universal for ranking or metric objectives.
Preference learning directly uses rankings, but margin/metric objectives are broader than preference learning.
Embedding spaces are common for metric objectives and contrastive retrieval, so common strength is defensible.
